results.title: Cencora AI Adoption Tracker | Larridin | AI Initiative Tracker
highlights:
Cencora (formerly AmerisourceBergen) is deploying AI across multiple business functions to enhance operational efficiency and improve healthcare delivery[1]. The pharmaceutical distribution company has integrated AI into systematic literature reviews, achieving 81-83% concordance with human reviewers[2][3]. Cencora has partnered with Infinitus to automate benefit verification processes, with AI agents handling calls to payors and achieving 97.8% first-pass approval rates[4]. The company is also investing $1 billion through 2030 to build AI-enabled distribution centers featuring autonomous mobile robots and advanced automation technology[5]. Leadership emphasizes AI's transformative potential across the healthcare ecosystem while maintaining a human-in-the-loop approach[6].

results.title: Amulya K
highlights:
Senior AI ML Engineer at [Cencora](https://www.linkedin.com/company/cencoraglobal)
...
I am a Senior AI/ML Engineer with 11+ years of experience building enterprise-scale solutions across healthcare and financial systems, with a strong focus on Generative AI and production-grade machine learning. My expertise lies in designing and deploying LLM-powered systems, including Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines that combine structured data (claims, billing, policy data) with unstructured sources (clinical notes, EOBs, transcripts) to deliver context-aware, reliable insights. I have hands-on experience building AI agents and multi-step workflows for real-world use cases such as claims validation, document processing, and operational automation. My work involves prompt engineering, embedding optimization, retrieval tuning, and building scalable AI microservices using Python and FastAPI. I have also developed NLP pipelines using transformers and spaCy for entity extraction, classification, and semantic understanding in domain-specific applications. From an engineering perspective, I bring strong experience in: MLOps (MLflow, Kubeflow, model lifecycle management) Data engineering (PySpark, Delta Lake, ETL pipelines) Cloud platforms (AWS, Azure) Vector databases (Pinecone, FAISS, Elasticsearch) Secure AI systems (RBAC, PII masking, HIPAA compliance) I enjoy working at the intersection of AI, data, and real-world business problems—translating complex workflows into scalable, production-ready solutions. I am currently open to opportunities in AI/ML Engineering, Generative AI, and backend systems involving intelligent automation.
...
### Senior AI ML Engineer at [Cencora](https://www.linkedin.com/company/cencoraglobal) (Current)
...
Designed and deployed RAG-based AI systems integrating claims data, EOBs, clinical notes, and policy documents Improved LLM response accuracy through prompt engineering, embedding tuning, and retrieval optimization Built AI agents to automate workflows such as claims validation, eligibility checks, and document verification Developed NLP pipelines for clinical and operational text using transformers and embedding-based approaches Exposed AI capabilities via secure APIs using FastAPI for enterprise applications Implemented MLOps pipelines (MLflow, Kubeflow) for model tracking, deployment, and monitoring Built scalable data pipelines using PySpark and Delta Lake for real-time and batch processing Ensured compliance with HIPAA standards using RBAC, encryption, and PII masking
...
I architected enterprise-grade RAG solutions that integrate both structured and unstructured healthcare data to enable accurate and scalable AI-driven insights. I built and optimized LLM orchestration pipelines using LangChain, LlamaIndex, and OpenAI/Azure OpenAI, significantly reducing hallucinations through advanced chunking strategies, embedding optimization, and semantic ranking techniques. I also developed intelligent AI agents for document classification, policy interpretation, and workflow automation, improving operational efficiency. In addition, I designed and deployed asynchronous FastAPI services to support large-scale AI workloads and implemented robust MLOps pipelines leveraging MLflow, Airflow, and Databricks for model tracking and deployment. Throughout these projects, I worked closely with compliance teams to ensure all AI systems adhered to HIPAA requirements and industry standards.

ai (artificial intelligence) • ai engineer • full stack development • python developer • python full stack developer • full stack developer • stack • engineer • python • python full stack development • developer
...
MCP vs RAG vs AI Agents
...
Everyone is talking about MCP, RAG, and AI Agents. Most people are still mixing them up. They’re not competing ideas. They solve very different problems at different layers of the stack.
...
MCP (Model Context Protocol) is about how LLMs use tools. Think of it as a standard interface between an LLM and external systems. Databases, file systems, GitHub, Slack, internal APIs. 

Instead of every app inventing its own glue code, MCP defines a consistent way for models to discover tools, invoke them, and get structured results back. MCP doesn’t decide what to do. It standardizes how tools are exposed.
...
RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) is about what the model knows at runtime. The model stays frozen. No retraining. When a user asks a question, a retriever fetches relevant documents (PDFs, code, vector DBs), and those are injected into the prompt.
...
RAG is great for:
...
- Internal knowledge bases
- Fresh or private data
- Reducing hallucinations
...
But RAG doesn’t take actions. It only improves answers.
...
AI Agents are about doing things. An agent observes, reasons, decides, acts, and repeats. It can call tools, write code, browse the internet, store memory, delegate tasks, and operate with different levels of autonomy.
...
a lightweight, PC-based gesture
...
's MediaPipe
...
It's incredibly satisfying to see machine learning models translate physical movements into clean, usable data streams. Building this was a great reminder of how accessible and powerful modern computer vision tools have become. #ComputerVision #Python #OpenCV #MediaPipe #MachineLearning #SoftwareEngineering #Tech

results.title: DrBA Kyle Pudenz
highlights:
I am an enterprise data, analytics, and AI executive who helps organizations convert complex information into trusted decisions, scalable products, and measurable business value. My work spans healthcare, supply chain, commercial analytics, AI/ML, knowledge graphs, governance, and enterprise technology at Fortune 10 scale. Today, I lead Data & AI Product commercialization within Cencora’s Enterprise Data, Analytics & AI team, part of the Global Data & Technology Solutions organization. My focus is translating enterprise data, analytics, AI, and subject-matter expertise into market-ready insight and decision products that deliver measurable customer and business outcomes. I combine business operating experience with emerging technology to build products that solve meaningful problems and operate at scale. Partnering closely with commercial, technology, analytics, legal, and business leaders, I help identify opportunities, shape product strategy, accelerate adoption, and create trusted products across external and enterprise data and AI portfolios. I hold a Doctor of Business Administration focused on corporate strategy and supply-chain design, an MBA, and multiple ASCM/APICS certifications. I have served as a member of the Gartner Data & Analytics Research Board and currently serve on the governing body of the New York Metro Gartner CDAO Community. I have also been recognized by CDO Magazine as one of its Data Leaders. Across my work, I value connecting with leaders across industries on how data, AI, governance, and product thinking can improve decision quality, create durable business value, and shape products customers trust.
...
I lead strategy, commercialization, and scaling of Cencora’s Data & AI product portfolio, converting proprietary enterprise data, analytics, AI, and subject-matter expertise into market-ready insight and decision products for customers, suppliers, and internal decision makers across a Fortune 10, $300B+ organization. I own portfolio direction, customer validation, governance, and go-to-market partnership across enterprise and external data and AI offerings, partnering with commercial, technology, analytics, legal, and business leaders to scale trusted products that create measurable customer and financial value. I build and lead product teams that convert enterprise data, analytics, and AI into durable, commercially viable solutions supporting revenue growth, operational leverage, and long-term competitive advantage.
...
Led Cencora’s Enterprise Data & Analytics function, owning enterprise data product strategy and roadmap while leading 30 FTE and 80 contractors across product management, data science, engineering, architecture, and governance. Partnered across technology and business leadership to standardize enterprise analytics architecture, governance, reusable data products, and platform patterns. Led the team responsible for Cencora’s first Databricks-based analytics custodian environment and foundational Neo4j knowledge graph capability, expanding governed data access beyond reporting to support advanced analytics and data science. Built and scaled an enterprise supply-chain-resiliency data and AI product used internally and piloted with suppliers and customers to anticipate disruptions, improve product availability, and support commercialization with $50M+ annual profit-impact potential. Launched FinOps analytics to map unallocated costs to customers and products, recovering $10M+ in annual taxes and improving executive visibility into margin, cost-to-serve, and product economics. Advised senior leadership and external stakeholders on policy, supply-chain risk, shortages, and tariff impacts, including engagement with Congressional committees, DoD, HHS, and White House advisors. Built an empowered, product-minded team culture recognized through employee inclusion results, strengthening accountability, collaboration, and outcome-driven delivery.

It feels incredibly rewarding to have successfully completed the Post Graduate Program in "Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning: Business Applications" from UT Austin's McCombs School of Business.  

This learning journey strengthened my understanding of how AI/ML solutions can be applied to real-world business problems through hands-on projects across the full machine learning lifecycle - from raw data preparation and model development to deployment for end-user consumption.

I gave a talk last week about Hermes Agent — an open-source AI assistant I've been running on a personal server for side projects and infrastructure work at MSK.
...
Hermes (by Nous Research ) is built around the idea that context is everything. It keeps persistent memory across sessions, supports tool integrations via MCP, and can take real actions on your behalf — not just answer questions. Think of it as a more autonomous, self-hosted alternative to something like Claude Code.

results.title: [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II | Wfh
highlights:
# [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II
...
Note: The job is a remote job and is open to candidates in USA. Cotiviti is a healthcare technology company focused on delivering better care at lower costs through advanced technology and data analytics. The Senior Generative AI Scientist II will work with a team to solve healthcare issues using machine learning and AI techniques, delivering value through innovative data solutions. Responsibilities • Work with key stakeholders within Research and Development as well as Business Operations, along with Product Management to assess the potential value and risks associated with business problems that have the potential to be solved using machine learning and Artificial Intelligence techniques • Develop an exploratory data analysis approach to verify the initial hypothesis associated with potential Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning use cases • Document your approach, thinking and results in standard approaches to allow other data scientists to collaborate with you on this work • Prepare your final trained model and develop a validation test set for QA • Work with ML Ops/Production operations to deploy your model into production and support them in monitoring model performance • Participate in design sessions to continuously develop and improve the Cotiviti machine learning platform • Participate in other data science initiatives, collaborating with your peers to support their projects • Participate in knowledge sharing sessions to bring new insights and technologies to the team • Provide End to End value-based solutions, including data pipeline, model creation and application for end user consumption • Complete all responsibilities as outlined in the annual performance review and/or goal setting • Complete all special projects and other duties as assigned • Must be able to perform duties with or without reasonable accommodation Skills • Graduate degree in a quantitative discipline such as Computer Science/Engineering, Statistics, Operations Research covering Advanced Statistics, Machine learning and AI • Experience with the latest techniques in natural language processing including transformers, fine-tuning LLMs, measuring/benchmarking and deploying LLMs with tools such as HuggingFace, Langchain, LLAMA/Mistral and OpenAI, vector databases • 5+ years of hands-on data science/AI experience, using typical machine learning and data science tools including pandas, scikit-learn, keras, nltk, and TensorFlow/PyTorch, GPU • Experience building production-grade machine learning deployments on AWS, Azure, or GCP • Experience working with Apache Spark™ and large-scale distributed datasets • Experience communicating technical concepts to non-technical and technical audiences is a plus • Flexibility to work with global teams as well geographically dispersed US based teams • Professional with ability to properly handle confidential information • Ability to work within a matrixed organization • Proficiency in all required skills and competencies above Benefits • Medical • Dental • Vision • Disability • Life insurance coverage • 401(k) savings plans • Paid family leave • 9 paid holidays per year • 17-27 days of Paid Time Off (PTO) per year, depending on specific level and length of service with Cotiviti. Company Overview • Cotiviti enables healthcare organizations to deliver better care at lower cost through advanced technology and data analytics that improve the quality and sustainability of healthcare in the United States. It was founded in 1979, and is headquartered in South Jordan, UT, US, with a workforce of 5001-10000 employees. Its website is Company H1B Sponsorship • Cotiviti has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, with 165 in 2025, 118 in 2024, 90 in 2023, 102 in 2022, 72 in 2021, 67 in 2020. Please note that this does not guarantee sponsorship for this specific role.

highlights: 2021, Pulsora the “Enterprise Sustainability Platform”, is a well-funded Silicon Valley software startup. It is dedicated to empowering purpose-driven enterprises to manage and improve their environmental, social, and governance (ESG) and overall sustainability footprint with an integrated, comprehensive, flexible, and innovative technology platform built for compliance, tracking, and insight. We are well funded, have amazing customers across industries and geographies, established strategic partnerships with leading ERP and consulting firms, and are growing fast! About the job We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced AI/ML Developer to join our team. The ideal candidate will be a specialist in the rapidly evolving field of Large Language Models (LLMs) and generative AI, with a strong background in developing, integrating, and optimizing complex agentic and RAG-based systems. What you will do LLM Integration & Development : Design, develop, and deploy production-grade applications leveraging various LLMs, context optimization, etc. Agentic Workflows : Architect and implement sophisticated, multi-step and multi-agent workflows using frameworks like LangChain and LangGraph. Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) : Build and optimize RAG pipelines, including implementing and managing embeddings, vector databases, and advanced rerankers to enhance response quality and relevance. Vibe Coding: Use code generation applications (e.g. Replit, Cursor, Google AI Studio, Git Hub Copilot in Agent mode, etc.) to create full applications (including frontend and backend), generate tests, perform testing and integrate them in the core product without writing any code. LLM Fine Tuning : Lead efforts in LLM fine-tuning (e.g., LoRA, QLoRA) for specific domain knowledge and tasks, and implement strategies for and efficiency. Prompt Engineering : Develop and refine advanced prompt engineering techniques to maximize model performance, consistency, and safety. Full-Stack Development : Own end-to-end implementation from frontend to the backend. Java (Plus) : Expose AI/LLM functionality written in Python using Java services, leverage multi-threading capabilities in Java to augment AI/LLM functionality developed in Python Code Quality & Productivity : Utilize AI-powered development tools (e.g., GitHub Copilot, etc.) to efficiently generate, refactor, and optimize high-quality code. Collaboration : Work closely with team leads managers, QA, product managers and team in the US (this will require the willingness to partially work US working hours) About you Undergraduate degree in Computer Science or similar Engineering field, advanced degree is a plus 5+ years of professional experience in software development, with a minimum of 2 years focused on AI/ML development, particularly with LLMs. Strong proficiency in Python and its relevant data science libraries (e.g., Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn). Proven experience integrating and working with major LLM APIs - both public and private/local, e.g., Gemini, OpenAI, Anthropic, Llama, Ollama, etc., including hands-on experience using techniques for LLM efficiency. At least 1 year of deep practical experience with LangChain and LangGraph for building complex LLM applications and agentic workflows using autonomous agents, tools, memory management, parallelization, etc. Solid understanding and implementation experience with RAG architectures, vector DBs, vector search, embeddings, and reranking mechanisms. Experience leveraging AI Copilot or similar generative AI coding tools for accelerated development, code generation, refactoring, optimization, and vibe coding to create integrated backend and frontend applications. Experience creating UI using React and integrating the UI with the backend using REST APIs is a big plus. Hands-on experience with Java, especially integrating Java modules with Python modules is a nice to have, but not necessary. A little more about how you like to work Strong collaboration
